compressG1Unchecked(bytes).details: UNCHECKED — see interface doc. Naming carries the contract:      callers must immediately follow with either a `keccak`-compare      against a pre-anchored identity OR a `verify(...)` call that      routes through PAIRING (which enforces subgroup membership).

compressG2Unchecked(bytes).details: UNCHECKED — see `compressG1Unchecked` doc for trust-anchor      binding requirement.

verify(bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes).details: Trust-anchor binding (sig/pk == registered/evidence identity) is      the CALLER's responsibility via compressG1Unchecked/compressG2Unchecked      — this is pure pairing over caller-supplied uncompressed points.

params.namespace: per-subject namespace (e.g. base‖_NOTARIZE, or PoP base)

compressG1Unchecked(bytes).notice: Compress a 128 B EIP-2537 G1 to 48 B zcash (MinSig). Pure;         on-curve/subgroup left to PAIRING. The caller binds the         result to its trust anchor.

compressG2Unchecked(bytes).notice: Compress a 256 B EIP-2537 G2 to 96 B zcash (c1-first, MinSig).

unionUnique(bytes,bytes).notice: union_unique(ns,msg) = single-byte LEB128(len ns) ‖ ns ‖ msg.         This is the single-byte LEB128 encoding (high bit cleared for         length < 128). The Rust mirror in `crates/bls` uses full LEB128         varint; as long as no namespace ever exceeds 127 bytes both         representations agree. Current namespaces are 23–32 bytes.

documentation.text:
Negated G2 generator in EIP-2537 form (256 B) — fixed protocol
 constant, identical to Eip2537ConformanceVectors.NEG_G2_GENERATOR.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes calldata,bytes calldata) pure returns (bytes memory)
function (bytes memory,bytes calldata) view returns (bytes memory)

documentation.text:
@notice union_unique(ns,msg) = single-byte LEB128(len ns) ‖ ns ‖ msg.
         This is the single-byte LEB128 encoding (high bit cleared for
         length < 128). The Rust mirror in `crates/bls` uses full LEB128
         varint; as long as no namespace ever exceeds 127 bytes both
         representations agree. Current namespaces are 23–32 bytes.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes memory,bytes memory) view returns (bytes memory)

documentation.text:
MODEXP(base, 1, p): ABI = I2OSP(64,32) ‖ I2OSP(1,32) ‖ I2OSP(48,32)
 ‖ base(64B) ‖ exp(0x01) ‖ p(48B); output 48 B = base mod p.

MAP_FP_TO_G1(16×0x00 ‖ fp48) -> 128 B G1 (EIP-2537).
 @dev Security note: this precompile is expected to clear the
      BLS12-381 G1 cofactor per RFC 9380 §6.6.3 (the EIP-2537
      field-to-curve annex prescribes
      `map_to_curve_simple_swu → iso_map → clear_cofactor`).
      As of this audit (2026-05-25):
        - reth/revm:    cleared (blst `src/map_to_g1.c`
                        `POINTonE1_times_minus_z` + `_dadd`)
        - go-ethereum:  cleared (gnark-crypto
                        `ecc/bls12-381/hash_to_g1.go::MapToG1`)
      If Fluent's L2 EVM client changes, re-verify cofactor
      clearing in the new implementation. Drift fails closed
      (PAIRING rejects non-prime-order point → verify returns
      false → slashing silently does NOT fire).

@notice Revert InfinityPoint if every byte of the EIP-2537 point is
         zero (PAIRING silently skips infinity pairs ⇒ forgeable).
